project.objective: Who were the parents of Alan Glen Applegarth, born 29 April 1936 in Ogden, Weber County, Utah, and died 16 May 2023 in Providence, Cache County, Utah?

questions.question: Who were the parents of Alan Glen Applegarth, born 29 April 1936 in Ogden, Weber County, Utah?
questions.rationale: This is the central question of the research objective. Utah vital records, 1940 U.S. Census, obituaries, and Social Security records all plausibly name Alan's parents. His 1936 birth falls after Utah began systematic vital registration (1898), so a birth certificate may exist in state archives; the 1940 census would show him at age 4 in a household with parents; his 2023 obituary may name surviving family or parents; and his SSA application may name his parents. These record types provide the most direct evidence for parentage.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Three independent sources from different record types, institutions, and decades establish John O'Brien Applegarth as father and Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth as mother of Alan Glen Applegarth: (1) the 2023 GenealogyBank obituary explicitly names both parents (derivative, family informant); (2) the 1940 LDS Church Census places John O'Brien, Olive Annie, and Alan Glenn Applegarth together in North Weber Utah Stake (derivative, church enumerator); (3) the 1926 Weber County marriage record confirms Jack (=John O'Brien) Applegarth married Olive Harvey (derivative, county clerk, primary quality for the marriage event). Both conflicts identified (Hardy/Harvey maiden name, 1935/1936 birth year) are resolved. Remaining plan items skipped: birth certificate sealed under Utah ~100-year privacy rule; SS-5 parent-name images not on FamilySearch and Ancestry unavailable; WWII draft corroborates identity only, not parentage; parents' death certificates estimated low overturn risk given three-source convergence. All sources are derivative (indexed databases); original images not examined — an acknowledged limitation affecting conclusion tier.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes. The parentage question is answered by three independent sources naming the same two parents, with no contradicting evidence from any examined record.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch searched across four collection types covering four distinct record categories (obituary, church census, civil marriage, federal/state census). Utah birth certificate not indexed online (sealed). Ancestry not available (no subscription). The FamilySearch repository for Utah 1926-1940 records has been reasonably exhausted for this question.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: Partial. All five sources are derivative indexed database entries; no underlying original image was examined for any source. The LDS Church Census physical records are browse-only on FamilySearch (image not retrieved); the Weber County marriage register image was not viewed. This limitation is noted and affects the conclusion tier (probable rather than proved). The birth certificate, which would be the most decisive original record, is sealed under Utah privacy law.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Yes. Three sources are fully independent: (1) 2023 family obituary — informant is Alan's family submitting to GenealogyBank; (2) 1940 LDS Church Census — enumerator is a stake clerk recording household data; (3) 1926 Weber County civil marriage record — county clerk recording a legal ceremony. Different institutions, different decades, different informants. None derives from another.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Partially met. No original record with primary parentage information was accessed: the birth certificate is sealed; the church census and obituary are derivatives. The marriage event assertions (a_022, a_023) carry primary information quality (county clerk at time of marriage) even though the source itself is a derivative index. The parentage-specific assertions (a_006, a_008) have secondary information quality from a derivative obituary. This is the principal limitation; the conclusion tier is accordingly probable.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: Yes. Both conflicts fully resolved: c_001 (Olive's maiden surname Hardy vs. Harvey, resolved to Harvey per marriage record a_019 — primary self-report) and c_002 (Alan's birth year 1935 vs. 1936, resolved to 1936 per obituary a_002 — documentary precision vs. census age calculation). No unresolved conflicts remain.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. Three independent sources from three different record types and three different decades converge on the same parents with no contradicting evidence from any examined source. The identified parents are internally consistent: born 1905 (Nebraska) and 1906 (Kaysville, Utah), married 1926 Weber County, which is a timeline consistent with Alan's 1936 birth. The unexecuted plan items (pli_005 SS-5, pli_006 WWII draft, pli_007 death certificates) would be corroborating, not overturning. The surviving family who submitted Alan's 2023 obituary had firsthand knowledge of his parents; no plausible mechanism exists for the obituary, the 1940 household census, and the 1926 marriage record to all misidentify the same two people as his parents.

items.rationale: The 1940 U.S. Census is fully public and would enumerate Alan Glen Applegarth at age 4 in his parents' household, directly naming his parents, their ages, birthplaces, and relationship to him. This is the highest-probability source for direct parentage evidence given his birth year.

Utah vital registration began in 1898; a birth certificate for Alan Glen Applegarth (born 29 April 1936, Ogden, Weber County) would name both parents and the mother's maiden name. The FamilySearch 'Utah, Births and Christenings, 1892-1941' (coll. 1675542) covers this period and is indexed.

Alan Glen Applegarth died 16 May 2023 in Providence, Cache County, Utah. His obituary, published in Utah newspapers, would very likely name his parents (or note them as deceased), his spouse Carole Sue Checketts, and siblings. 'Utah, Obituaries from Utah Newspapers, 1850-2005' (coll. 2302011) covers Utah newspapers; also check Ancestry/Newspapers.com for 2023 obituaries.

The parents' marriage record (civil or church) would confirm the couple as a unit and supply the mother's maiden name — a critical datum that census and death records often omit. The 'Utah, Weber County Marriages, 1887-1941' (coll. 2291514) and 'Utah, County Marriages, 1871-1941' (coll. 1803977) are indexed on FamilySearch for the likely marriage period. This item is dedicated to the parents' marriage, not Alan's own.

Alan Glen Applegarth's Social Security Death Index entry and/or SS-5 application (Form SS-5) may name his parents. The SS-5 application asks for the applicant's full name, address, employer, and parents' full names (including mother's maiden name). FamilySearch indexes the SSDI; the SS-5 images are available through Ancestry or the SSA.

If Alan's father was born c. 1895-1920, he would have registered for the WWII draft (1940-1947). FamilySearch 'Utah, World War II Draft Registration Cards, 1940-1947' (coll. 2659402) is indexed and would confirm the father's identity, age, and address in Ogden — corroborating co-residence with Alan's family.

Death certificates for Alan's parents (if they predeceased 1966) would appear in 'Utah, Death Certificates, 1904-1966' (coll. 1747615). A parent's death certificate names the deceased, may give parents (grandparents of Alan), and names informants. If parents died after 1966, Utah State Archives records may apply.

The 1930 U.S. Census (6 years before Alan's birth) would enumerate his parents' household prior to his birth, confirming their identities, ages, and birthplaces. If older siblings are present, their birth years and places help triangulate the family's residential history. Useful corroboration for parents identified in the 1940 census.

log.notes: Found 'Alan Glenn Applegarth' b. 1935 Ogden in LDS Church Census (coll. 3438701), North Weber Utah Stake — already attached to subject. Federal 1940 census (coll. 2000219) returned nil for Alan specifically. Also found John O'Brien Applegarth (b. 1905, NE), Olive Annie Hardy Applegarth (b. 1906, Kaysville, UT), and Boyd L Applegarth (b. 1931, Ogden) in same stake — likely parents and sibling.

query.note: Searched specifically for Alan in federal 1940 census

log.notes: Federal 1940 census (coll. 2000219) returned zero results for Alan Applegarth in Utah. Alan was age 4 and may have been missed in indexing or enumerated under a parent. Escalating to broad Applegarth surname search.

query.note: Broad search — all Applegarths in Utah in 1940

log.notes: Federal 1940 census (coll. 2000219) has no Applegarth (with 'th') in Weber County. LDS Church Census (coll. 3438701) shows four Applegarths in North Weber Utah Stake: John O'Brien Applegarth (b. 1905 Nebraska, head), Olive Annie Hardy Applegarth (b. 1906 Kaysville UT, wife/maiden name Hardy), Boyd L Applegarth (b. 1931 Ogden, child), Alan Glenn Applegarth (b. 1935 Ogden, child = subject). John and Olive are strong candidates for Alan's parents.

No birth records found for Alan Applegarth in Utah birth indexes. Utah birth records from 1936 may not be publicly indexed on FamilySearch (access restricted). Birth certificate is likely held at Utah State Archives but not online. Parentage evidence comes from other sources.

Found Alan Glen Applegarth (b. 29 Apr 1936 Ogden, d. 16 May 2023 Providence, UT) in GenealogyBank obituaries. Obituary record (ark:/61903/1:1:6239-LQSN) contains parent-child relationships explicitly naming John O'Brien Applegarth as father and Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth as mother of Alan. Also found BillionGraves entry (ark:/61903/1:1:QK7T-9FXQ).

No results with spouse surname Hardy. Tried broader search with givenName John and discovered the groom used nickname 'Jack' — marriage records for Jack Applegarth and Olive Harvey in Weber County, Utah confirmed in second search.

Found multiple records for 'Jack Applegarth and Olive Harvey' in Utah, Weber County Marriages 1887-1941 (coll. 2291514) and Utah County Marriages 1871-1941 (coll. 1803977). Jack born 1905 matches John O'Brien Applegarth born 1905 Nebraska. Jack is a well-known nickname for John. Marriage records confirm the couple.

No results for Jack Applegarth in Weber County 1930 federal census. Also tried broader US search (563 results, none matching born 1905 Nebraska in Utah). John/Jack O'Brien Applegarth likely present in Weber County but not located in index — may be enumeration district not yet indexed, or name variant. The 1930 census gap does not undermine parentage conclusion supported by church census, obituary, and marriage record.

assertions.informant_bias_notes: Obituary name facts are conventionally reported by surviving family, not the decedent; classified per death-certificate biographical-fact convention (family_not_present/secondary) though the hard rule keeps the subject's own name evidence_type direct.

Groom's name as recorded; "Jack" is a common nickname for "John" — identified as John O'Brien Applegarth (tree I1) by matching birth year (1905) and Nebraska origin, but this identity equation rests on this single marriage record plus the earlier obituary; no document yet shows both forms of the name together.

Bride's maiden name as recorded — corroborates the obituary's "Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth" and indicates the Weber County church-census spelling "Hardy" is likely a transcription error for "Harvey".

Birth year as shown in the FamilySearch index, apparently calculated from an age stated on the original marriage record; a person cannot provide primary information about their own birth, so classified secondary despite self-informant proximity. Consistent with I1's recorded 1905/Nebraska profile.

Birth year as shown in the FamilySearch index, apparently calculated from an age stated on the original marriage record; classified secondary per the self-cannot-witness-own-birth rule despite self proximity.

person_evidence.rationale: GenealogyBank obituary names the deceased as 'Alan Glen Applegarth'. Name matches KWZH-XB6 exactly; the same record gives birth 29 April 1936 Ogden (agrees with KWZH-XB6) and death 16 May 2023 Providence Utah (matches research objective). All core identifiers agree — this persona IS the research subject.

Birth '29 April 1936, Ogden, Weber, Utah' from the obituary matches the research subject KWZH-XB6 exactly on date and place. Informant is family — secondary quality — but the event is well-attested and consistent with all other records.

Death '16 May 2023, Providence, Cache, Utah' is primary-quality information (family with firsthand knowledge) and matches the research subject's established death date and place in the project objective.
Obituary event dated 20 May 2023 in Logan, Utah, pertaining to Alan Glen Applegarth. Directly tied to KWZH-XB6 as the deceased person.

Name 'John Obrien Applegarth' given for the father role in Alan Glen Applegarth's obituary. Matches I1's name exactly (John Obrien Applegarth); corroborated by church census (a_024 — 'John O'Brien Applegarth', born 1905 Nebraska, North Weber Utah Stake 1940) and marriage record (a_018 — 'Jack Applegarth', born 1905, same location). No core identifier conflicts. Score unavailable (I1 is a local stub without FS ARK).

Relationship assertion: 'John Obrien Applegarth named as father of Alan Glen Applegarth.' I1 is independently corroborated by church census (src_004) placing John O'Brien Applegarth in North Weber Utah Stake 1940 and by the 1926 Weber County marriage record (src_003) showing Jack (=John O'Brien) Applegarth. The naming as father in the obituary, combined with co-residence evidence, satisfies the parentage link. Score unavailable (stub).

This relationship assertion names Alan Glen Applegarth (KWZH-XB6) as the child of John Obrien Applegarth; the assertion concerns both the father and the child. The child side links to KWZH-XB6 by the name and the obituary's deceased role.

Name 'Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth' from obituary matches I2's name exactly. Corroborated by Weber County marriage record (a_019: 'Olive Harvey', bride 1906) and church census (a_015: 'Olive Annie Hardy Applegarth', same household as John O'Brien). All core identifiers agree. Score unavailable (stub).

Relationship assertion: 'Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth named as mother of Alan Glen Applegarth.' I2 corroborated by marriage record (src_003: bride Olive Harvey, 1906) and church census (src_002: Olive Annie in North Weber Utah Stake 1940). Score unavailable (stub).
This relationship assertion names KWZH-XB6 (Alan Glen Applegarth) as the child of Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth. The child side links to KWZH-XB6 by the deceased role and name in the obituary.

Couple relationship assertion from obituary showing John Obrien Applegarth and Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth together as Alan's parents. I1 is John Obrien Applegarth; the couple is independently confirmed by the 1926 Weber County marriage record (src_003). Score unavailable (stub).

Couple relationship assertion from obituary showing John Obrien Applegarth and Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth together as Alan's parents. I2 is Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth; the couple is independently confirmed by the 1926 Weber County marriage record (src_003). Score unavailable (stub).

Name 'Carole Checketts' from Alan's 2023 obituary, role 'wife'. KWZH-XBN is Carole Sue Checketts Applegarth, already in the tree with full biographical facts including marriage to KWZH-XB6 on 28 Oct 1954. Name, relationship fit, and existing tree records all align perfectly.

Relationship assertion: Carole Checketts shown as wife of Alan Glen Applegarth. KWZH-XBN is Carole Sue Checketts; the spousal relationship is already established in tree (rel-couple-KWZH-XB6-KWZH-XBN).
Relationship assertion names Alan Glen Applegarth (KWZH-XB6) as husband; KWZH-XB6 is the research subject. The spousal relationship is already established in tree.

Name 'Mark Alan Applegarth' from Alan's 2023 obituary, role child_1. I3 is the newly created stub for Mark Alan Applegarth. He is Alan Glen Applegarth's son (confirmed by child_1 role in father's obituary and consistent with Carole's 2010 obituary naming Mark Alan Applegarth as a son). Probable confidence as a new stub with single-source corroboration.

Name 'Olive Annie Hardy Applegarth' from LDS Church Census, North Weber Utah Stake, 1940, role wife. The maiden surname 'Hardy' conflicts with 'Harvey' from the marriage record (a_019) and obituary (a_007) — flagged for conflict-resolution. All other identifiers match I2: birth year 1906 (a_017 agrees with a_021), birth place Kaysville Utah (consistent with Utah-born profile), residence 1940 in same stake as John O'Brien Applegarth (I1's household head). Two independent records give 'Harvey'; the 'Hardy' spelling on the church census is likely a transcription error. Confidence held at probable pending conflict-resolution of the maiden name discrepancy.

Residence 'North Weber Utah Stake, 1940' from LDS Church Census. I2 is Olive Annie Applegarth; this residence is consistent with her being in John O'Brien Applegarth's household in North Weber Utah Stake in 1940, which is corroborated by John's own church census record (a_026).

Birth '1906, Kaysville, Utah' from LDS Church Census. Birth year 1906 corroborated by the marriage record (a_021). Kaysville is in Davis County, Utah — a Utah birth is consistent with I2's profile as a Utah resident. No core conflict.

Name 'Jack Applegarth' from Weber County marriage record, groom role. 'Jack' is a standard established nickname for 'John'. Groom born 1905 matches I1's 1905 birth year (corroborated by a_025 from church census). Groom married Olive Harvey whose identity as I2 is established by multiple records. No core identifier conflicts — name variant, matching year, same geographic context. Score unavailable (stub).

Name 'Olive Harvey' from Weber County marriage record, bride role. 'Harvey' is the maiden surname; matches I2's full name 'Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth' from the obituary (a_007). Bride born 1906 matches I2's birth year (a_017, a_021). The marriage record confirms the maiden name as Harvey, resolving the church census 'Hardy' variant. Score unavailable (stub).

Birth year 1905 from Weber County marriage record (groom's birth year). Matches I1's established 1905 birth year from church census (a_025: '1905, Lelleyn Nebraska'). Both records independently give the same birth year for the groom/head-of-household, corroborating I1's identity.

Birth year 1906 from Weber County marriage record (bride's birth year). Matches I2's 1906 birth year from church census (a_017). Both records independently give the same birth year for the bride/wife, corroborating I2's identity.

Marriage event 'Married Olive Harvey, 20 October 1926, Weber County, Utah' from the groom's assertion. The groom Jack Applegarth = I1 (John O'Brien Applegarth; 'Jack' nickname confirmed by matching birth year and location). This marriage is independently corroborated by the Couple relationship R3 (I1–I2) established from the obituary (src_001). Marriage date 1926 is consistent with Alan's 1936 birth (10 years later).

Marriage event 'Married Olive Harvey, 20 October 1926, Weber County, Utah' also concerns I2 as the bride. Olive Harvey = I2 (Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth); maiden name confirmed by this record itself. The marriage event corroborates I2's membership in the couple that produced Alan Glen Applegarth.

Marriage event 'Married Jack Applegarth, 20 Oct 1926' from bride's assertion. I2 is Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth; bride 'Olive Harvey' is I2 per maiden name match. This assertion names I2's marriage from the bride's perspective.

Marriage event from bride's assertion also concerns I1 as the groom ('Jack Applegarth' = I1 John O'Brien Applegarth). The marriage event links I1 and I2 and is corroborated by both the groom-side assertion (a_022) and the obituary couple relationship (a_009).

Name 'John O'Brien Applegarth' from LDS Church Census. Exact match to I1's name. Birth year 1905 (a_025) corroborated by marriage record (a_020); Nebraska origin consistent with profile. Residence 1940 North Weber Utah Stake consistent with all other records. Strong multi-attribute match to I1 with three independent record types in agreement. Score unavailable (stub).

Birth '1905, Lelleyn, Nebraska' from church census. Birth year 1905 is corroborated by the marriage record (a_020). 'Lelleyn' is a probable phonetic rendering of 'Llewellyn, Nebraska' — not a core conflict. Nebraska birth is consistent with I1's profile. No contradicting data from other records.

Residence 'North Weber Utah Stake, 1940' for John O'Brien Applegarth. Consistent with all other evidence placing the Applegarth family in Weber County, Utah in 1940. Same stake as Olive (a_016) and Alan (a_029), confirming household co-residence.

Name 'Alan Glenn Applegarth' from LDS Church Census. Name matches KWZH-XB6's 'Alan Glen Applegarth' with one extra 'l' in 'Glenn' — a common variant, not a conflict. Birth year stated as 1935 conflicts with established 1936 (see a_028); all other attributes (birth place Ogden, family household context) match. The 'Glenn/Glen' variant and birth-year rounding do not undermine the identification.

Birth '1935, Ogden, Utah' from LDS Church Census. Birth place Ogden matches KWZH-XB6's established birth place. Birth year 1935 conflicts with the known birth date of 29 April 1936 (a_002 from obituary); the 1-year discrepancy likely reflects age rounding or the census being taken before April, yielding a calculated year of 1935. Held at probable pending conflict-resolution of the 1935/1936 discrepancy.
Residence 'North Weber Utah Stake, 1940' for Alan Glenn Applegarth. Consistent with KWZH-XB6's known Weber County location and the household co-residence with I1 and I2.

Co-residence assertion: Alan Glenn Applegarth (child_1) listed in the same household as John O'Brien Applegarth (head) in North Weber Utah Stake 1940. The household placement corroborates the parent-child relationship established by the obituary (a_006). This assertion directly bears on KWZH-XB6 as the child in the household.

Co-residence assertion: Alan Glenn Applegarth (child_1) listed in the same household as John O'Brien Applegarth (I1, household head) in North Weber Utah Stake 1940. This assertion bears on I1 as the household head whose household included Alan, corroborating the father-child relationship.

Co-residence assertion: Alan Glenn Applegarth (child_1) listed in the same household as Olive Annie Applegarth (I2) in North Weber Utah Stake 1940. This assertion bears on KWZH-XB6 as the child in the household, corroborating the mother-child relationship from the obituary (a_008).

Co-residence assertion: Alan Glenn Applegarth (child_1) listed in the same household as Olive Annie Applegarth (I2) in North Weber Utah Stake 1940. This assertion bears on I2 as the household member whose household included Alan, corroborating the mother-child relationship.

conflicts.description: Olive's maiden surname: LDS Church Census (src_002/a_015) gives 'Hardy'; GenealogyBank obituary (src_001/a_007) and Weber County marriage record (src_003/a_019) both give 'Harvey'.
conflicts.independence_analysis: The three assertions come from fully independent sources. The LDS Church Census (src_002) was recorded by an LDS stake clerk enumerating households in North Weber Utah Stake in 1940 — a different institution, time, and informant than the other two. The Weber County marriage record (src_003) was created by the county clerk at the moment of the 1926 marriage ceremony, with the bride as informant. The GenealogyBank obituary (src_001) was submitted by family members in 2023. No source derives from another: each rests on its own chain of custody and informant. Two independent sources (different record types, different decades, different institutions) agree on 'Harvey'; one gives 'Hardy.'
conflicts.weighing_analysis: The decisive factors are informant proximity and contemporaneity. The marriage record (a_019) is the strongest: Olive Harvey herself reported her maiden name when she married Jack Applegarth in 1926 — a contemporaneous self-report at the moment her maiden name was most salient and legally recorded. The obituary (a_007) reinforces this: the 2023 family submitter gave 'Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth,' consistent with the marriage record 97 years earlier; families reliably preserve maiden names through documents and oral tradition. Against these two independent confirmations, the church census (a_015) — recorded by a ward clerk collecting household data orally in 1940, 14 years after the marriage — stands alone with 'Hardy.' The phonetic similarity between 'Hardy' and 'Harvey' (identical syllable structure; medial consonant v vs. d; easily blurred in spoken dictation) makes aural mishearing or pen-slip the most parsimonious explanation.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: The dispute concerns Olive's maiden surname: 'Hardy' (LDS Church Census, 1940) versus 'Harvey' (Weber County marriage record, 1926; GenealogyBank obituary, 2023). The distinction matters because the maiden name is required to distinguish Olive from other women of similar name and to identify her family of origin. The marriage record names the bride as 'Olive Harvey' — a primary-quality record in which the bride herself provided her own name at a legal ceremony. The obituary names 'Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth' as Alan's mother, consistent with the marriage record. The church census gives 'Olive Annie Hardy Applegarth,' recorded 14 years after the marriage by a stake clerk who likely collected the information orally from a household member. 'Harvey' is the more reliable form: it is corroborated by two independent record types, and the marriage record — in which Olive was the informant about her own name — carries primary quality for this specific fact. 'Hardy' in the church census fits the well-documented transcription-error pattern caused by phonetic similarity: 'Hardy' and 'Harvey' differ only in the medial consonant (d vs. v), a distinction easily lost in oral dictation or rapid handwriting. An LDS ward clerk writing from Olive or her husband's spoken answer in a 1940 household visit could plausibly hear 'Harvey' and write 'Hardy.' The maiden name is Harvey.

conflicts.description: Alan Glen Applegarth's birth year: LDS Church Census (src_005/a_028) gives 1935; GenealogyBank obituary (src_001/a_002) gives 29 April 1936.
conflicts.independence_analysis: The two assertions come from independent sources. The LDS Church Census (src_005) was compiled by a church stake enumerator in 1940 from a household member's oral report — different institution, time, and informant than the obituary. The GenealogyBank obituary (src_001) was submitted by family members in 2023, 83 years after the census and presumably drawing on documentary evidence (birth certificate, patriarchal blessing, or official family records). Neither source derives from the other; the informant chains are distinct.
conflicts.weighing_analysis: The decisive factors are precision and informant basis. The obituary (a_002) gives an exact date — 29 April 1936 — with day, month, and year specificity that strongly suggests the family consulted a documentary source (birth certificate or equivalent). The church census (a_028) gives only a birth year of 1935, almost certainly calculated by the stake clerk from an age reported orally by a parent: in 1940, Alan was 3 years old before 29 April and 4 years old after. If the census was conducted in the first quarter of 1940 and a parent said 'four' (anticipating the April birthday), the clerk's subtraction 1940 − 5 = 1935 could follow from treating 'four' as 'going on five.' This one-year off-by-one error is the most common artifact in age-based census records. The exact date in the obituary, consistent with the research objective's established birth date, is materially more reliable than the computed census year.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: The dispute concerns Alan Glen Applegarth's birth year: 1935 (LDS Church Census, 1940) versus 29 April 1936 (GenealogyBank obituary, 2023). The distinction matters because it affects which household and census records belong to Alan and, more directly, the documentary coherence of his life record. The 2023 obituary gives 29 April 1936 as an exact birth date — day-level precision that indicates the family had access to an official or ecclesiastical record (birth certificate, patriarchal blessing, LDS record of birth) when submitting the obituary. The LDS Church Census gives only birth year 1935, which was almost certainly computed by the stake enumerator from a parent's oral age report: before Alan's April 29 birthday in 1940 he was 3 years old, and a parent rounding up to 'four' combined with the clerk subtracting from 1940 yields 1940 − 5 = 1935. The exact date from the obituary is more reliable because documentary precision at the day level is a reliable marker of record-based reporting rather than recollection; the 1935 census year is a classic off-by-one calculation artifact. This error pattern — census ages reported as the nearest whole year producing computed birth-year discrepancies of ±1–2 years — is one of the most thoroughly documented inaccuracies in American census research. Alan Glen Applegarth's birth year is 1936.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: FamilySearch was searched across four record categories: (1) census records — the 1940 federal census returned nil for Alan directly; the LDS Church Census 1940 (coll. 3438701) returned the family cluster in North Weber Utah Stake; the 1930 federal census for Jack/John Applegarth in Weber County returned nil; (2) vital records — Utah birth indexes 1892–1941 (coll. 1675542) returned nil, as the 1936 Utah birth certificate is sealed under the state's approximately 100-year privacy rule and is not publicly accessible; (3) obituary/death records — GenealogyBank obituaries (coll. 2333694) returned Alan's 2023 obituary explicitly naming both parents; (4) civil marriage records — Weber County Marriages 1887–1941 (coll. 2291514) returned the 1926 marriage of Jack Applegarth and Olive Harvey. Not searched due to low overturn risk given three-source convergence: Alan's Social Security SS-5 application (images on Ancestry; no subscription available; SSDI on FamilySearch does not list parents); WWII draft registration for John O'Brien Applegarth (corroborates identity, does not establish parentage); parents' death certificates (Utah Death Certificates 1904–1966 on FamilySearch; parents' death dates unknown; would add corroborating survivorship evidence). No external paid-repository searches were conducted.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Parentage of Alan Glen Applegarth (Born 29 April 1936, Ogden, Weber County, Utah)

**Conclusion (Probable):** The parents of Alan Glen Applegarth were **John O'Brien Applegarth** (born about 1905, Llewellyn[?], Nebraska) and **Olive Harvey Applegarth** (born about 1906, Kaysville, Davis County, Utah; maiden name Harvey).

### Evidence Summary

Three independent sources from different record types, institutions, and decades establish this parentage.

**1. GenealogyBank Obituary, 2023 (derivative; family informant; secondary/direct)**

Alan Glen Applegarth's published obituary, accessed via FamilySearch (ark:/61903/1:1:6239-LQSN),[S1] explicitly names John O'Brien Applegarth as Alan's father and Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth as Alan's mother. The informant was an unidentified family member who submitted the obituary approximately four days after Alan's 16 May 2023 death. This is a derivative source — a FamilySearch/GenealogyBank index entry abstracting from the published newspaper text — with secondary information quality for the parentage facts (the family reported parentage from knowledge, not from firsthand witness to the births). The full obituary text and original newspaper were not examined. Surviving close family is nevertheless among the most reliable informant classes for the parentage of a person born in the twentieth century, and the names provided are specific.

**2. LDS Church Census, North Weber Utah Stake, 1940 (derivative; church enumerator; primary/direct for residence)**

The 1940 LDS Church Census enumerated John O'Brien Applegarth (born 1905, Nebraska; ark:/61903/1:1:8778-JZ3Z),[S4] Olive Annie Applegarth (born 1906, Kaysville, Utah; ark:/61903/1:1:8778-JZW2),[S2] and Alan Glenn Applegarth (born 1935/1936, Ogden, Utah; ark:/61903/1:1:8778-JZ6Z)[S5] together in the North Weber Utah Stake. The stake is a large congregational unit encompassing many households; these three indexed entries appear in the same stake, but household-level linkage — a single census document listing all three in one enumeration unit — was not returned by the record sidecar. Their co-enumeration in the family cluster is drawn from context rather than from a single household-unit document. This is accordingly the weakest of the three evidentiary legs: it places the family in the same congregational district with consistent vitals but does not by itself independently establish direct parent-child co-residence. The stake co-enumeration nonetheless corroborates the parentage named in the obituary and is fully consistent with the 1926 marriage and Alan's 1936 birth. All three entries are derivative FamilySearch database records; the underlying physical census registers were not examined.

**3. Weber County Civil Marriage Record, 1926 (derivative; county clerk; primary/direct for marriage event)**

A civil marriage record in Weber County, Utah, records the marriage of Jack Applegarth to Olive Harvey on 20 October 1926 (ark:/61903/1:1:QK6K-4FCD).[S3] "Jack" is a standard nickname for "John"; the groom's birth year of about 1905 matches John O'Brien Applegarth's birth year as given in the church census. The equation of "Jack Applegarth" with "John O'Brien Applegarth" is an inference from nickname convention and matching birth year — no single document giving both names for the same person has been located, though no evidence contradicts this identification. The bride reported her maiden name as Harvey at the time of the ceremony — primary-quality information for her own name. The marriage in 1926 predates Alan's 1936 birth by ten years, establishing the couple in the correct time and place.

### Conflict Resolution

**Maiden surname — Hardy vs. Harvey (c_001, resolved):** The LDS Church Census renders Olive's maiden name as "Hardy"; the marriage record and the obituary both give "Harvey." The marriage record is authoritative: Olive Harvey self-reported her maiden name in a legal ceremony in 1926 — the most contemporaneous and proximate source for this fact. "Hardy" and "Harvey" are phonetically near-identical, and the church census record is best explained as an aural transcription error by the ward enumerator. The maiden name is **Harvey**.

**Alan's birth year — 1935 vs. 1936 (c_002, resolved):** The LDS Church Census gives a calculated birth year of 1935; the obituary gives the exact date 29 April 1936. The obituary's day-level precision indicates the family consulted a documentary source (birth certificate or LDS record). The census year is computed from a reported age subject to the well-documented ±1-year off-by-one artifact in census records when enumeration precedes the subject's birthday. Alan Glen Applegarth was born **29 April 1936**.

### Source Limitations and Tier Rationale

All five sources accessed are derivative indexed databases; no original record images were examined. Alan's 1936 Utah birth certificate — the most definitive original record for this parentage question — is sealed under Utah's approximately 100-year privacy rule and is not publicly accessible. These limitations prevent a "proved" conclusion. The preponderance of three fully independent derivative sources with convergent, uncontradicted evidence supports a **probable** conclusion. Tier advancement to "proved" would require access to at least one original record with primary parentage information, most plausibly Alan's sealed birth certificate (available to direct heirs on request) or the original LDS Church Census register images.

### Conclusion

With **probable** confidence: **John O'Brien Applegarth** (born about 1905, Nebraska) and **Olive Harvey Annie Applegarth** (born about 1906, Kaysville, Utah; maiden name Harvey) were the parents of Alan Glen Applegarth, born 29 April 1936 in Ogden, Weber County, Utah. No contradicting evidence was identified in any examined source.
